verify.name: FIFO Buffer
verify.code:
import cocotb
from cocotb import *
import random

# Define the parameters for the FIFO instance
# We will use a small, manageable size for testing
FIFO_DEPTH = 8
FIFO_WIDTH = 8

@cocotb.test()
async def test_fifo_operations(dut):
    """
    Tests the basic functionality of the FIFO, including reset, normal read/write,
    and edge cases (full/empty).
    """
    # Set up the DUT parameters (if the module allowed parameter setting via cocotb,
    # we would do it here. Since it's defined in Verilog, we assume the instantiation
    # handles the parameters, but we will use the defined constants for logic.)
    
    # Initialize signals
    cocotb.set_float_from_hex("clk", 0)
    cocotb.set_float_from_hex("rst", 0)
    cocotb.set_float_from_hex("wr_en", 0)
    cocotb.set_float_from_hex("rd_en", 0)
    cocotb.set_float_from_hex("data_in", 0)

    # --- 1. Clock Generation ---
    cocotb.start_elaboration()
    cocotb.wait(10 * cocotb.CLOCK_PERIOD, cycle=True) # Wait for initial setup

    # --- 2. Test Case: Reset ---
    cocotb.log.info("--- Starting Test 1: Reset ---")
    dut.rst.value = 1
    await cocotb.wait(2, cycle=True)
    
    dut.rst.value = 0
    await cocotb.wait(2, cycle=True)
    
    # Check initial state after reset
    assert dut.full.value == 0, "After reset, full should be 0"
    assert dut.empty.value == 1, "After reset, empty should be 1"
    
    # --- 3. Test Case: Normal Write and Read Cycle ---
    cocotb.log.info("--- Starting Test 2: Normal Write/Read Cycle ---")
    
    # Write 3 items
    for i in range(3):
        data = i + 1
        cocotb.log.info(f"Writing data: {data}")
        dut.data_in.value = data
        dut.wr_en.value = 1
        dut.rd_en.value = 0
        await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)
        
        # Check state after write
        assert dut.empty.value == 0, f"After write {i}, empty should be 0"
        assert dut.full.value == 0, "Should not be full yet"
        
        dut.wr_en.value = 0
        await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)

    # Read 2 items
    for i in range(2):
        await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True) # Wait for clock edge
        read_data = dut.data_out.value
        cocotb.log.info(f"Read data: {read_data}")
        
        # Check read data correctness
        expected_data = i + 1
        assert read_data == expected_data, f"Expected read data {expected_data}, got {read_data}"
        
        dut.rd_en.value = 1
        dut.wr_en.value = 0
        await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)
        
        # Check state after read
        assert dut.empty.value == 0, "Should not be empty yet"
        assert dut.full.value == 0, "Should not be full yet"
        
        dut.rd_en.value = 0
        await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)

    # --- 4. Test Case: Filling and Emptying Edge Cases ---
    cocotb.log.info("--- Starting Test 3: Edge Cases (Full/Empty) ---")
    
    # A. Fill the FIFO (Write DEPTH items)
    cocotb.log.info("Filling FIFO to capacity...")
    for i in range(FIFO_DEPTH):
        data = i + 10 # Use distinct data for tracking
        dut.data_in.value = data
        dut.wr_en.value = 1
        dut.rd_en.value = 0
        await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)
        
        # Check state
        if i < FIFO_DEPTH - 1:
            assert dut.full.value == 0, "Should not be full yet"
        else:
            assert dut.full.value == 1, "FIFO must be full now"
            
        dut.wr_en.value = 0
        await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)
        
    # B. Attempt Overwrite (Should fail/be ignored if logic is correct)
    cocotb.log.info("Attempting write when full (should fail)...")
    dut.data_in.value = 999 # Dummy data
    dut.wr_en.value = 1
    dut.rd_en.value = 0
    await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)
    # The internal logic handles this by checking `!full`, so we just ensure the state doesn't change unexpectedly.
    assert dut.full.value == 1, "Still full after attempted write"
    dut.wr_en.value = 0
    await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)

    # C. Empty the FIFO (Read DEPTH items)
    cocotb.log.info("Draining FIFO to empty...")
    for i in range(FIFO_DEPTH):
        await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)
        read_data = dut.data_out.value
        
        # Check read data correctness (should match the data written in step A)
        expected_data = i + 10
        assert read_data == expected_data, f"Expected read data {expected_data}, got {read_data}"
        
        dut.rd_en.value = 1
        dut.wr_en.value = 0
        await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)
        
        # Check state
        if i < FIFO_DEPTH - 1:
            assert dut.empty.value == 0, "Should not be empty yet"
        else:
            assert dut.empty.value == 1, "FIFO must be empty now"
            
        dut.rd_en.value = 0
        await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)

    # D. Attempt Underflow (Should fail/be ignored)
    cocotb.log.info("Attempting read when empty (should fail)...")
    dut.rd_en.value = 1
    dut.wr_en.value = 0
    await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)
    # Check that the empty flag remains high and no unexpected data is read
    assert dut.empty.value == 1, "Still empty after attempted read"
    dut.rd_en.value = 0
    await cocotb.wait(1, cycle=True)

    cocotb.log.info("FIFO tests completed successfully.")

# Note: To run this test, you must compile the Verilog module and execute:
# cocotb-bci cocotb-scdut -m fifo.v -t $TESTNAME

verify.name: Simple ALU
verify.code:
import cocotb
from cocotb import clock, start, waveform

# Define the clock frequency (e.g., 100 MHz, period = 10 ns)
CLOCK_PERIOD = "10ns"

@cocotb.test()
async def alu_test(dut):
    """
    Testbench for the 8-bit ALU module.
    Tests addition, subtraction, AND, and OR operations, covering normal and edge cases.
    """
    # Initialize inputs
    a_val = 0
    b_val = 0
    op_val = 0
    
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 1. Reset Test (Initial State Check)
    # Since the DUT is combinational, we assume inputs are stable initially.
    # We set inputs to zero and check the initial result.
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    cocotb.log.info("--- Starting Reset Test ---")
    dut.a.value = 0
    dut.b.value = 0
    dut.op.value = 0
    await start_cycle()
    
    # Expected result for (0, 0, ADD) is 0.
    assert dut.result.value == 0, f"Expected result 0 after reset, got {dut.result.value}"
    assert dut.zero.value == 1, "Zero flag should be set after (0, 0) operation"
    cocotb.log.info("Reset Test Passed.")

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 2. Normal Operation Test: Addition (op = 00)
    # Test case: a = 10, b = 20. Expected result = 30.
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    cocotb.log.info("\n--- Starting Addition Test (a+b) ---")
    a_val = 10
    b_val = 20
    op_val = 2'b00
    
    dut.a.value = a_val
    dut.b.value = b_val
    dut.op.value = op_val
    await start_cycle()
    
    expected_result = a_val + b_val
    assert dut.result.value == expected_result, \
        f"ADD failed: Expected {expected_result}, got {dut.result.value}"
    assert dut.zero.value == 0, "Zero flag should be clear after non-zero addition"
    cocotb.log.info(f"Addition Test Passed: {a_val} + {b_val} = {expected_result}")

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 3. Edge Case Test: Subtraction and Zero Result (op = 01)
    # Test case: a = 5, b = 5. Expected result = 0.
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    cocotb.log.info("\n--- Starting Subtraction Test (a-b) ---")
    a_val = 5
    b_val = 5
    op_val = 2'b01
    
    dut.a.value = a_val
    dut.b.value = b_val
    dut.op.value = op_val
    await start_cycle()
    
    expected_result = a_val - b_val
    assert dut.result.value == expected_result, \
        f"SUB failed: Expected {expected_result}, got {dut.result.value}"
    assert dut.zero.value == 1, "Zero flag must be set when result is zero"
    cocotb.log.info(f"Subtraction Test Passed: {a_val} - {b_val} = {expected_result}")

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 4. Edge Case Test: Bitwise Operations (AND/OR)
    # Test case: a = 0b11110000, b = 0b10101010
    # AND (op=10): 0b10100000 (160)
    # OR (op=11): 0b11111010 (250)
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    cocotb.log.info("\n--- Starting Bitwise Test (AND/OR) ---")
    a_val = 0b11110000
    b_val = 0b10101010
    
    # Test AND (op = 10)
    dut.a.value = a_val
    dut.b.value = b_val
    dut.op.value = 2'b10
    await start_cycle()
    expected_and = a_val & b_val
    assert dut.result.value == expected_and, \
        f"AND failed: Expected {expected_and}, got {dut.result.value}"
    assert dut.zero.value == 0, "Zero flag should be clear after AND"
    cocotb.log.info(f"AND Test Passed: {a_val} & {b_val} = {expected_and}")

    # Test OR (op = 11)
    dut.a.value = a_val
    dut.b.value = b_val
    dut.op.value = 2'b11
    await start_cycle()
    expected_or = a_val | b_val
    assert dut.result.value == expected_or, \
        f"OR failed: Expected {expected_or}, got {dut.result.value}"
    assert dut.zero.value == 0, "Zero flag should be clear after OR"
    cocotb.log.info(f"OR Test Passed: {a_val} | {b_val} = {expected_or}")


# Helper function to wait for the combinational logic to settle
async def start_cycle():
    """Waits for one clock cycle period to allow combinational logic to settle."""
    await cocotb.wait(CLOCK_PERIOD)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Clock Generation Setup
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
@cocotb.clock(CLOCK_PERIOD, "clk")
async def clock_cycle(dut):
    """Generates a simple clock signal."""
    dut.clk.value = 1
    await cocotb.wait(CLOCK_PERIOD / 2)
    dut.clk.value = 0
    await cocotb.wait(CLOCK_PERIOD / 2)

verify.name: Shift Register
verify.code:
import cocotb
from cocotb.triggers import RisingEdge, FallingEdge, Timer

# Define the parameters used in the DUT (N=8)
N_BITS = 8

@cocotb.test()
async def test_shift_reg_operations(dut):
    """
    Testbench for the shift_reg module.
    Covers reset, loading, and shifting operations.
    """
    # Initialize all inputs to default values
    cocotb.set_float_from_env("CLOCK_PERIOD", "10ns")
    
    # --- Setup and Reset Test Case ---
    # 1. Assert initial state (all inputs low, data should be zero)
    dut.rst.value = 1
    dut.load.value = 0
    dut.shift.value = 0
    dut.data_in.value = 0
    
    # Wait for one clock cycle to ensure reset propagates
    await RisingEdge(dut.clk)
    
    # Check if the register has been reset to 0
    await cocotb.wait_for_value(dut.data_out.integer, 0, timeout=100)
    assert dut.data_out.integer == 0, "Error: Data out should be 0 after reset."
    assert dut.serial_out.integer == 0, "Error: Serial out should be 0 after reset."
    
    # Deassert reset
    dut.rst.value = 0
    await RisingEdge(dut.clk)
    
    # --- Loading Test Case (Normal Operation) ---
    # 2. Load a specific pattern (e.g., 0b10101010)
    test_data_load = 0b10101010
    dut.load.value = 1
    dut.shift.value = 0
    dut.data_in.value = test_data_load
    
    await RisingEdge(dut.clk)
    
    # Check if the data was successfully loaded
    await cocotb.wait_for_value(dut.data_out.integer, test_data_load, timeout=100)
    assert dut.data_out.integer == test_data_load, f"Error: Data out expected {test_data_load}, got {dut.data_out.integer}"
    assert dut.serial_out.integer == (test_data_load >> (N_BITS - 1)) & 1, "Error: Serial out mismatch after load."
    
    # Deassert load
    dut.load.value = 0
    await RisingEdge(dut.clk)

    # --- Shifting Test Case (Sequence and Edge Case) ---
    # 3. Shift the data three times.
    # Initial state: 10101010
    # Shift 1: 01010100 (MSB=1 drops, LSB=0 added)
    # Shift 2: 00101000
    # Shift 3: 00010000
    
    dut.shift.value = 1
    
    # Shift 1
    await RisingEdge(dut.clk)
    expected_data_1 = 0b01010100
    await cocotb.wait_for_value(dut.data_out.integer, expected_data_1, timeout=100)
    assert dut.data_out.integer == expected_data_1, f"Error: Shift 1 failed. Expected {expected_data_1}, got {dut.data_out.integer}"
    assert dut.serial_out.integer == 0, "Error: Serial out mismatch after Shift 1."
    
    # Shift 2
    await RisingEdge(dut.clk)
    expected_data_2 = 0b00101000
    await cocotb.wait_for_value(dut.data_out.integer, expected_data_2, timeout=100)
    assert dut.data_out.integer == expected_data_2, f"Error: Shift 2 failed. Expected {expected_data_2}, got {dut.data_out.integer}"
    
    # Shift 3 (Final check)
    await RisingEdge(dut.clk)
    expected_data_3 = 0b00010000
    await cocotb.wait_for_value(dut.data_out.integer, expected_data_3, timeout=100)
    assert dut.data_out.integer == expected_data_3, f"Error: Shift 3 failed. Expected {expected_data_3}, got {dut.data_out.integer}"
    
    # Cleanup: Ensure all control signals are low
    dut.load.value = 0
    dut.shift.value = 0
    dut.data_in.value = 0
